subtext: kerem can't parse the system. architect proposes three routes to fix legibility.
how to separate talking about the system from talking inside it. architect misunderstands, then corrects. ASIDE chosen.
the grammar produces syntax, not content. Sontag's 'art is something' reframes the question.
three options tested and rejected. claims are imported, observations are self-reference, instructions are a different project. all three miss the point.
first tool that exists outside the system but inside the document.

summary: back to the problem / sourced fragments
subtext: restating the problem cleanly. new idea: fragments sourced from outside, not generated. meaningful content subjected to arbitrary rules.
ban on reframing clichés and dramatic colons in architect writing.
counting the layers. document, operations, narrative, metalog, highlights. changelog sits outside.
ASCII diagrams are fine, styled lists are not diagrams. diagrams must show connections prose cannot.
left-margin agent that groups conversation into threads with summaries and tags.
revisiting the sourced fragments idea without the slop. meaningful input + arbitrary judgment. is that enough or just a shredder?
feeding the system its own first metalog entry. agreeing to build sourced Writer + snapshots.
questioning inherited alive/dead metaphor. building an Editor agent that revises interface language without changing system data. third kind of agent: transforms presentation, not document.
"cycle 8" sounds like a title. too many numbers. proposing a taxonomist agent to own the naming layer. Editor handles tone, taxonomist handles structure.
the source pool was selected without principle. a poet would curate with intention, thinking about sound, overlap, length, and what is missing.
